feed.title:
Hao Zhai
翟昊
feed.subtitle: This is Hao Zhai's personal site. He is a PhD student from Institute of Automation, CAS. His research interest includes computer vision and connectomics.

entry.title: Guidelines for My Personal Site
entry.summary: If you feel confuse about post-centered theme, please follow this guidelines. I would like to give you a brief description about the architecture of my site. To visit my profile page, please check left sidebar and click my photo or “About Me”. Holistic Architecture flowchart TD h["HOME"] -- "type" --> t1["CATEGORIES"] h["HOME"] -- "keyword" --> t2["TAGS"] h["HOME"] -- "timel...

entry.title: Awesome EM Datasets
entry.summary: The original github repo: https://github.com/JackieZhai/awesome-em-datasets Screenshot of our github repository. Awesome logo.

entry.title: Silence for Thought: Special Interneuron Networks in the Human Brain

link.title: Silence for Thought: Special Interneuron Networks in the Human Brain

entry.summary: Scientists map prominent differences in the neural circuits of mice, monkeys, and human. The analysis of the human brain is a central goal of neuroscience. However, for methodological reasons, research has largely focused on model organisms, in particular the mouse. Now, neuroscientists gained novel insights on human neural circuitry using tissue obtained from neurosurgical interventions. Thre...

entry.title: A Browsable Petascale Reconstruction of the Human Cortex

link.title: A Browsable Petascale Reconstruction of the Human Cortex

entry.summary: In January 2020 we released the fly “hemibrain” connectome — an online database providing the morphological structure and synaptic connectivity of roughly half of the brain of a fruit fly (Drosophila melanogaster). This database and its supporting visualization has reframed the way that neural circuits are studied and understood in the fly brain. While the fruit fly brain is small enough to att...

entry.title: Unveiling the Biggest and Most Detailed Map of the Fly Brain Yet

link.title: Unveiling the Biggest and Most Detailed Map of the Fly Brain Yet

entry.summary: Janelia and Google scientists have constructed the most complete map of the fly brain ever created, pinpointing millions of connections between 25,000 neurons. Now, a wiring diagram of the entire brain is within reach. In a darkened room in Ashburn, Virginia, rows of scientists sit at computer screens displaying vivid 3-D shapes. With a click of a mouse, they spin each shape to examine it fr...
